_comment: Example Claude Code hooks fragment: crystallize first, then Operator crystal-bridge. Trust/review hooks in the harness UI before relying on them. Hook subprocesses do not necessarily inherit your interactive shell PATH, so this invokes agent-crystallize via 'npx -y' rather than assuming a bare binary is resolvable. If you have agent-crystallize installed globally (or at a known absolute path) and want to skip npx's resolve/version-check overhead, replace 'npx -y @stewie-sh/agent-crystallize' with that binary's absolute path (e.g. /usr/local/bin/agent-crystallize or $(npm root -g)/@stewie-sh/agent-crystallize/bin/...). 'operator' below is assumed to already be resolvable (installed/on PATH); use an absolute path to your checkout's ./operator if not.

hooks.command: npx -y @stewie-sh/agent-crystallize hook --harness claude-code --event SessionStart; operator crystal-bridge --event SessionStart --harness claude
npx -y @stewie-sh/agent-crystallize hook --harness claude-code --event Stop; operator crystal-bridge --event Stop
